description.task: Implement an event bus using the Observer pattern. Publishers emit named events; subscribers register interest in specific event types. When an event is published, all registered subscribers for that event type are notified in registration order.
instructions: subscribe() adds the handler to the event type's list using computeIfAbsent
unsubscribe() removes the exact handler reference from the event type's list
publish() iterates over all handlers for eventType and calls each with the payload
publish() returns the number of handlers notified; returns 0 for unknown event types
Events with no subscribers are silently ignored (return 0)
description.hint: In publish(), use subscribers.getOrDefault(eventType, List.of()). Copy the list before iterating to avoid ConcurrentModificationException if a handler unsubscribes itself. Return the size of the handler list.
description.expectedOutput:
Notified: 2
[H1: Order#42, H2: Order#42]
After unsub count: 1

description.task: Implement a SortContext class using the Strategy pattern. The context delegates sorting to interchangeable strategy implementations: BubbleSort, QuickSort proxy, and natural ordering. Allow switching strategies at runtime.
instructions: NaturalOrderStrategy.sort() creates a copy of the input list, uses Collections.sort(), and returns it
setStrategy() replaces the current strategy at runtime
Neither sort strategy should modify the original input list
Both strategies must return a new list in ascending order for the same input
description.hint: NaturalOrderStrategy.sort(): List<Integer> copy = new ArrayList<>(input); Collections.sort(copy); return copy. executeSort(): return strategy.sort(input).

description.task: Implement a text editor command system using the Command pattern. Commands like Insert and Delete encapsulate both the action and its inverse (undo). A CommandHistory stack supports multi-level undo and redo operations.
instructions: DeleteCommand.execute() saves content.substring(start, end) to 'deleted' field, then calls content.delete(start, end)
DeleteCommand.undo() calls content.insert(start, deleted) to restore the removed text
redo() pops from redoStack, calls execute(), and pushes to undoStack
executeCommand() must clear the redoStack to prevent stale redo history
undo() and redo() are no-ops when their respective stacks are empty
description.hint: DeleteCommand.execute(): deleted = content.substring(start, end); content.delete(start, end). redo(): if (!redoStack.isEmpty()) { cmd = redoStack.pop(); cmd.execute(); undoStack.push(cmd); }
description.expectedOutput:
Hello World
Hello
Hello World
Hello
Hello World
